title: podman container RUNLABEL variable substituition has regressed
body: When building an image that uses run label variables like $IMAGE and $PWD, they are discarded.

comments.body: Runlabel is not the problem. If I try to build such image buildah already substitute the variables with nothing. You can just run an inpect and compare the label, e.g. this works `LABEL RUN podman run -v PWD:/test IMAGE`

This should also work: `LABEL RUN podman run -v \$PWD:/test \$IMAGE`.
I think it is expected that buildah already runs variable substitution. @nalind @flouthoc @rhatdan correct?

If you're asking if $ needs to be escaped to prevent attempts to evaluate it at build time, yes.  If we've regressed, an example case and a known-good version would be helpful in figuring out what broke.
@nalind is correct, escaping is required for variables ... IMAGE and NAME also do NOT require a $
